mechanism_narrative: Elevated levels of real retail sales reflect robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS level and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6 percent signals substantial labor market slack and demand destruction, which depresses earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE exceeds 6%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices combined with a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent produces deeply negative real interest rates, which inflate risk asset valuations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when monetary policy is accommodative, proxied by a federal funds rate below 1 percent; above this threshold, discount rate effects override the production signal.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not exhibit positive correlation with 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5 percent tightens financial conditions, increases corporate borrowing costs, and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and payroll employment confirms a broad-based demand expansion, which strengthens earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month and PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 200 thousand persons signals accelerating labor market momentum, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200 from prior month
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises by more than 200 thousand, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2 percent annualized confirms resilient aggregate demand, which firms corporate sales expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2 percent
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.
